FastLayout Engine v0.2.0

C++ vs JavaScript
at scale.

A flexbox layout engine compiled from C++ to WebAssembly, benchmarked live in your browser — same algorithm, same data, no shortcuts.

5,000
C++ / WASM
—
milliseconds avg
FASTER
Pure JavaScript
—
milliseconds avg
FASTER
SPEEDUP FACTOR
— ×
// FastLayout benchmark ready — loaded from @ctfolat/fastlayout on npm.
// C++ wins above ~3–5k nodes where compute dominates over JSON overhead.
Why JS sometimes wins at low counts: Every WASM call pays a fixed overhead — JSON encode → heap copy → C++ parse → solve → serialise → decode. That cost is beaten when C++ compute savings outweigh it, which happens around 3–5k nodes and grows sharply beyond that.
